#!/usr/bin/env bash
# GitHub Actions Matrix Generator
# Generates build matrices from configuration describing OS options, language versions, and feature flags

set -euo pipefail

main() {
    if [[ $# -ne 1 ]]; then
        echo "Error: Configuration JSON required as argument" >&2
        return 1
    fi

    local config="$1"

    # Validate JSON input
    if ! jq . <<< "$config" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "Error: Invalid JSON configuration" >&2
        return 1
    fi

    # Extract required and optional fields
    local os_array version_array language_array include_rules exclude_rules
    local fail_fast max_parallel max_matrix_size

    # Get arrays with defaults
    os_array=$(jq -r '.os // empty | @json' <<< "$config" 2>/dev/null) || os_array="[]"
    version_array=$(jq -r '.version // empty | @json' <<< "$config" 2>/dev/null) || version_array="[]"
    language_array=$(jq -r '.language // empty | @json' <<< "$config" 2>/dev/null) || language_array="[]"

    # Get include/exclude rules
    include_rules=$(jq '.include // []' <<< "$config")
    exclude_rules=$(jq '.exclude // []' <<< "$config")

    # Get configuration options
    fail_fast=$(jq '.fail-fast // null' <<< "$config")
    max_parallel=$(jq '.max-parallel // null' <<< "$config")
    max_matrix_size=$(jq '.max-matrix-size // 256' <<< "$config")

    # Validate required fields exist
    if [[ -z "$(jq '.os' <<< "$config" 2>/dev/null || echo '')" ]] && [[ $(jq '.include | length' <<< "$config") -eq 0 ]]; then
        echo "Error: 'os' field is required when 'include' is empty" >&2
        return 1
    fi

    # Build the matrix
    local matrix
    matrix=$(build_matrix "$os_array" "$version_array" "$language_array" "$include_rules" "$exclude_rules" "$max_matrix_size")

    # Add configuration options to matrix
    if [[ "$fail_fast" != "null" ]]; then
        matrix=$(jq ".fail-fast = $fail_fast" <<< "$matrix")
    fi

    if [[ "$max_parallel" != "null" ]]; then
        matrix=$(jq ".max-parallel = $max_parallel" <<< "$matrix")
    fi

    echo "$matrix"
}

build_matrix() {
    local os_array="$1"
    local version_array="$2"
    local language_array="$3"
    local include_rules="$4"
    local exclude_rules="$5"
    local max_size="$6"

    # Start with empty include array
    local matrix='{"include": []}'

    # Generate cartesian product from base arrays
    local os_items version_items language_items
    os_items=$(jq -r '.[]? // empty' <<< "$os_array" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
    version_items=$(jq -r '.[]? // empty' <<< "$version_array" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
    language_items=$(jq -r '.[]? // empty' <<< "$language_array" 2>/dev/null || echo "")

    # If we have arrays, generate cartesian product
    if [[ -n "$os_items" ]] || [[ -n "$version_items" ]] || [[ -n "$language_items" ]]; then
        matrix=$(generate_cartesian_product "$os_array" "$version_array" "$language_array" "$exclude_rules")
    fi

    # Add include rules
    matrix=$(add_include_rules "$matrix" "$include_rules")

    # Validate matrix size
    local matrix_size
    matrix_size=$(jq '.include | length' <<< "$matrix")
    if [[ $matrix_size -gt $max_size ]]; then
        echo "Error: Matrix size ($matrix_size) exceeds maximum ($max_size)" >&2
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$matrix"
}

generate_cartesian_product() {
    local os_array="$1"
    local version_array="$2"
    local language_array="$3"
    local exclude_rules="$4"

    local matrix='{"include": []}'

    # Parse arrays and generate combinations
    local os_values version_values language_values
    os_values=$(jq -r '.[]? // empty' <<< "$os_array" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
    version_values=$(jq -r '.[]? // empty' <<< "$version_array" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
    language_values=$(jq -r '.[]? // empty' <<< "$language_array" 2>/dev/null || echo "")

    # Generate all combinations
    while IFS= read -r os; do
        [[ -z "$os" ]] && continue
        while IFS= read -r version; do
            [[ -z "$version" ]] && continue
            while IFS= read -r language; do
                [[ -z "$language" ]] && continue

                # Build combination object
                local combo='{'
                [[ -n "$os" ]] && combo="$combo\"os\":\"$os\","
                [[ -n "$version" ]] && combo="$combo\"version\":\"$version\","
                [[ -n "$language" ]] && combo="$combo\"language\":\"$language\","
                combo="${combo%,}}"

                # Check if should exclude
                if ! should_exclude "$combo" "$exclude_rules"; then
                    matrix=$(jq ".include += [$combo]" <<< "$matrix")
                fi
            done <<< "$language_values"
        done <<< "$version_values"
    done <<< "$os_values"

    echo "$matrix"
}

add_include_rules() {
    local matrix="$1"
    local include_rules="$2"

    local rule_count
    rule_count=$(jq '.include | length' <<< "$include_rules" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)

    for ((i = 0; i < rule_count; i++)); do
        local rule
        rule=$(jq ".include[$i]" <<< "$include_rules")
        matrix=$(jq ".include += [$rule]" <<< "$matrix")
    done

    echo "$matrix"
}

should_exclude() {
    local combo="$1"
    local exclude_rules="$2"

    local rule_count
    rule_count=$(jq '.exclude | length' <<< "$exclude_rules" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)

    for ((i = 0; i < rule_count; i++)); do
        local rule
        rule=$(jq ".exclude[$i]" <<< "$exclude_rules")

        # Check if combo matches exclude rule
        if matches_rule "$combo" "$rule"; then
            return 0  # Should exclude
        fi
    done

    return 1  # Should not exclude
}

matches_rule() {
    local combo="$1"
    local rule="$2"

    local rule_keys
    rule_keys=$(jq -r 'keys[]' <<< "$rule")

    while IFS= read -r key; do
        [[ -z "$key" ]] && continue

        local combo_val rule_val
        combo_val=$(jq -r ".$key // \"\"" <<< "$combo")
        rule_val=$(jq -r ".$key // \"\"" <<< "$rule")

        # If either is empty, skip comparison
        if [[ -z "$combo_val" ]] || [[ -z "$rule_val" ]]; then
            continue
        fi

        # If values don't match, rule doesn't match
        if [[ "$combo_val" != "$rule_val" ]]; then
            return 1
        fi
    done <<< "$rule_keys"

    return 0  # All matched fields are identical
}

main "$@"

        return 1

    # Extract required and optional fields
    local os_array version_array language_array include_rules exclude_rules
    local fail_fast max_parallel max_matrix_size

    os_array=$(jq -r '.os // empty | @json' <<< "$config" 2>/dev/null) || os_array="[]"

    language_array=$(jq -r '.language // empty | @json' <<< "$config" 2>/dev/null) || language_array="[]"

    # Get include/exclude rules
    include_rules=$(jq '.include // []' <<< "$config")
    exclude_rules=$(jq '.exclude // []' <<< "$config")

    fi

    local os_array="$1"
    local version_array="$2"
    local language_array="$3"
    local include_rules="$4"
    local exclude_rules="$5"
    local max_size="$6"

    # Start with empty include array
    local matrix='{"include": []}'

    local os_items version_items language_items
    os_items=$(jq -r '.[]? // empty' <<< "$os_array" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
    version_items=$(jq -r '.[]? // empty' <<< "$version_array" 2>/dev/null || echo "")

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# GitHub Actions Matrix Generator
# Generates build matrices from configuration describing OS options, language versions, and feature flags

set -euo pipefail

main() {
    if [[ $# -ne 1 ]]; then
        echo "Error: Configuration JSON required as argument" >&2
        return 1
    fi

    local config="$1"

    # Validate JSON input
    if ! jq . <<< "$config" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "Error: Invalid JSON configuration" >&2
        return 1
    fi

    # Extract base arrays and configuration
    local os_array version_array language_array include_rules exclude_rules
    local fail_fast max_parallel max_matrix_size

    # Get arrays (empty arrays if not present)
    os_array=$(jq -c '.os // []' <<< "$config")
    version_array=$(jq -c '.version // []' <<< "$config")
    language_array=$(jq -c '.language // []' <<< "$config")

    # Get include/exclude rules
    include_rules=$(jq '.include // []' <<< "$config")
    exclude_rules=$(jq '.exclude // []' <<< "$config")

    # Get configuration options
    fail_fast=$(jq '.["fail-fast"] // null' <<< "$config")
    max_parallel=$(jq '.["max-parallel"] // null' <<< "$config")
    max_matrix_size=$(jq '.["max-matrix-size"] // 256' <<< "$config")

    # Validate required fields exist
    local has_os_array has_include_rules
    has_os_array=$(jq -c '.os | length > 0' <<< "$config" 2>/dev/null || echo false)
    has_include_rules=$(jq -c '.include | length > 0' <<< "$config" 2>/dev/null || echo false)

    if [[ "$has_os_array" == "false" ]] && [[ "$has_include_rules" == "false" ]]; then
        echo "Error: 'os' field is required when 'include' is empty" >&2
        return 1
    fi

    # Build the matrix
    local matrix
    matrix=$(build_matrix "$os_array" "$version_array" "$language_array" "$include_rules" "$exclude_rules" "$max_matrix_size")

    # Add configuration options to matrix
    if [[ "$fail_fast" != "null" ]]; then
        matrix=$(jq ".\"fail-fast\" = $fail_fast" <<< "$matrix")
    fi

    if [[ "$max_parallel" != "null" ]]; then
        matrix=$(jq ".\"max-parallel\" = $max_parallel" <<< "$matrix")
    fi

    echo "$matrix"
}

build_matrix() {
    local os_array="$1"
    local version_array="$2"
    local language_array="$3"
    local include_rules="$4"
    local exclude_rules="$5"
    local max_size="$6"

    # Start with empty include array
    local matrix='{"include": []}'

    # Get lengths of base arrays
    local os_len version_len language_len
    os_len=$(jq 'length' <<< "$os_array")
    version_len=$(jq 'length' <<< "$version_array")
    language_len=$(jq 'length' <<< "$language_array")

    # Generate cartesian product from base arrays
    if [[ $os_len -gt 0 ]] || [[ $version_len -gt 0 ]] || [[ $language_len -gt 0 ]]; then
        matrix=$(generate_cartesian_product "$os_array" "$version_array" "$language_array" "$exclude_rules")
    fi

    # Add include rules
    matrix=$(add_include_rules "$matrix" "$include_rules")

    # Validate matrix size
    local matrix_size
    matrix_size=$(jq '.include | length' <<< "$matrix")
    if [[ $matrix_size -gt $max_size ]]; then
        echo "Error: Matrix size ($matrix_size) exceeds maximum ($max_size)" >&2
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$matrix"
}

generate_cartesian_product() {
    local os_array="$1"
    local version_array="$2"
    local language_array="$3"
    local exclude_rules="$4"

    local matrix='{"include": []}'

    # Get lengths
    local os_len version_len language_len
    os_len=$(jq 'length' <<< "$os_array")
    version_len=$(jq 'length' <<< "$version_array")
    language_len=$(jq 'length' <<< "$language_array")

    # If all arrays are empty, return empty matrix
    if [[ $os_len -eq 0 ]] && [[ $version_len -eq 0 ]] && [[ $language_len -eq 0 ]]; then
        echo "$matrix"
        return 0
    fi

    # Iterate through all combinations
    for ((i = 0; i < os_len; i++)); do
        local os_val
        os_val=$(jq -r ".[$i]" <<< "$os_array")

        for ((j = 0; j < version_len; j++)); do
            local version_val
            version_val=$(jq -r ".[$j]" <<< "$version_array")

            for ((k = 0; k < language_len; k++)); do
                local language_val
                language_val=$(jq -r ".[$k]" <<< "$language_array")

                # Build combination object
                local combo='{}'
                [[ -n "$os_val" ]] && combo=$(jq ".os = \"$os_val\"" <<< "$combo")
                [[ -n "$version_val" ]] && combo=$(jq ".version = \"$version_val\"" <<< "$combo")
                [[ -n "$language_val" ]] && combo=$(jq ".language = \"$language_val\"" <<< "$combo")

                # Check if should exclude
                if ! should_exclude "$combo" "$exclude_rules"; then
                    matrix=$(jq ".include += [$combo]" <<< "$matrix")
                fi
            done
        done
    done

    echo "$matrix"
}

add_include_rules() {
    local matrix="$1"
    local include_rules="$2"

    local rule_count
    rule_count=$(jq 'length' <<< "$include_rules")

    for ((i = 0; i < rule_count; i++)); do
        local rule
        rule=$(jq ".[$i]" <<< "$include_rules")
        matrix=$(jq ".include += [$rule]" <<< "$matrix")
    done

    echo "$matrix"
}

should_exclude() {
    local combo="$1"
    local exclude_rules="$2"

    local rule_count
    rule_count=$(jq 'length' <<< "$exclude_rules")

    for ((i = 0; i < rule_count; i++)); do
        local rule
        rule=$(jq ".[$i]" <<< "$exclude_rules")

        # Check if combo matches exclude rule
        if matches_rule "$combo" "$rule"; then
            return 0  # Should exclude
        fi
    done

    return 1  # Should not exclude
}

matches_rule() {
    local combo="$1"
    local rule="$2"

    local rule_keys
    rule_keys=$(jq -r 'keys[]' <<< "$rule")

    local all_match=true
    while IFS= read -r key; do
        [[ -z "$key" ]] && continue

        local combo_val rule_val
        combo_val=$(jq -r ".$key // \"\"" <<< "$combo")
        rule_val=$(jq -r ".$key // \"\"" <<< "$rule")

        # If either is empty, skip comparison
        if [[ -z "$combo_val" ]] || [[ -z "$rule_val" ]]; then
            continue
        fi

        # If values don't match, rule doesn't match
        if [[ "$combo_val" != "$rule_val" ]]; then
            all_match=false
            break
        fi
    done <<< "$rule_keys"

    if [[ "$all_match" == "true" ]]; then
        return 0  # All matched fields are identical
    fi
    return 1
}

main "$@"

    local matrix='{"include": []}'

    # Get lengths
    local os_len version_len language_len
    os_len=$(jq 'length' <<< "$os_array")
    version_len=$(jq 'length' <<< "$version_array")
    language_len=$(jq 'length' <<< "$language_array")

    # If all arrays are empty, return empty matrix
    if [[ $os_len -eq 0 ]] && [[ $version_len -eq 0 ]] && [[ $language_len -eq 0 ]]; then
        echo "$matrix"
        return 0
    fi

    # Normalize to at least 1 for iteration purposes
    local os_iter version_iter language_iter
    os_iter=$os_len
    [[ $os_iter -eq 0 ]] && os_iter=1
    version_iter=$version_len
    [[ $version_iter -eq 0 ]] && version_iter=1
    language_iter=$language_len
    [[ $language_iter -eq 0 ]] && language_iter=1

    # Iterate through all combinations
    for ((i = 0; i < os_iter; i++)); do
        local os_val=""
        if [[ $os_len -gt 0 ]]; then
            os_val=$(jq -r ".[$i]" <<< "$os_array")
        fi

        for ((j = 0; j < version_iter; j++)); do
            local version_val=""
            if [[ $version_len -gt 0 ]]; then
                version_val=$(jq -r ".[$j]" <<< "$version_array")
            fi

            for ((k = 0; k < language_iter; k++)); do
                local language_val=""
                if [[ $language_len -gt 0 ]]; then
                    language_val=$(jq -r ".[$k]" <<< "$language_array")
                fi

                # Build combination object
                local combo='{}'
                [[ -n "$os_val" ]] && combo=$(jq ".os = \"$os_val\"" <<< "$combo")
                [[ -n "$version_val" ]] && combo=$(jq ".version = \"$version_val\"" <<< "$combo")
                [[ -n "$language_val" ]] && combo=$(jq ".language = \"$language_val\"" <<< "$combo")

                # Check if should exclude
                if ! should_exclude "$combo" "$exclude_rules"; then
                    matrix=$(jq ".include += [$combo]" <<< "$matrix")
                fi
            done
        done
    done

    echo "$matrix"
}

add_include_rules() {
    local matrix="$1"
    local include_rules="$2"

    local rule_count
    rule_count=$(jq 'length' <<< "$include_rules")

    for ((i = 0; i < rule_count; i++)); do
        local rule
        rule=$(jq ".[$i]" <<< "$include_rules")
        matrix=$(jq ".include += [$rule]" <<< "$matrix")
    done

    echo "$matrix"
}

should_exclude() {
    local combo="$1"
    local exclude_rules="$2"

    local rule_count
    rule_count=$(jq 'length' <<< "$exclude_rules")

    for ((i = 0; i < rule_count; i++)); do
        local rule
        rule=$(jq ".[$i]" <<< "$exclude_rules")

        # Check if combo matches exclude rule
        if matches_rule "$combo" "$rule"; then
            return 0  # Should exclude
        fi
    done

    return 1  # Should not exclude
}

matches_rule() {
    local combo="$1"
    local rule="$2"

    local rule_keys
    rule_keys=$(jq -r 'keys[]' <<< "$rule")

    local all_match=true
    while IFS= read -r key; do
        [[ -z "$key" ]] && continue

        local combo_val rule_val
        combo_val=$(jq -r ".$key // \"\"" <<< "$combo")
        rule_val=$(jq -r ".$key // \"\"" <<< "$rule")

        # If either is empty, skip comparison
        if [[ -z "$combo_val" ]] || [[ -z "$rule_val" ]]; then
            continue
        fi

        # If values don't match, rule doesn't match
        if [[ "$combo_val" != "$rule_val" ]]; then
            all_match=false
            break
        fi
    done <<< "$rule_keys"

    if [[ "$all_match" == "true" ]]; then
        return 0  # All matched fields are identical
    fi
    return 1
}

main "$@"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# GitHub Actions Matrix Generator
# Generates build matrices from configuration describing OS options, language versions, and feature flags

set -euo pipefail

main() {
    if [[ $# -ne 1 ]]; then
        echo "Error: Configuration JSON required as argument" >&2
        return 1
    fi

    local config="$1"

    # Validate JSON input
    if ! jq . <<< "$config" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "Error: Invalid JSON configuration" >&2
        return 1
    fi

    # Build the matrix using jq
    local matrix
    matrix=$(jq '
        # Extract arrays and config options
        (.os // []) as $os |
        (.version // []) as $version |
        (.language // []) as $language |
        (.include // []) as $include |
        (.exclude // []) as $exclude |
        (.["fail-fast"] // null) as $failfast |
        (.["max-parallel"] // null) as $maxparallel |
        (.["max-matrix-size"] // 256) as $maxsize |

        # Check for required fields
        if (($os | length) == 0 and ($include | length) == 0) then
            error("os field is required when include is empty")
        else . end |

        # Generate cartesian product
        (
            if (($os | length) == 0 and ($version | length) == 0 and ($language | length) == 0) then
                []
            else
                [
                    (($os | if length == 0 then [null] else . end | .[]) as $o |
                    ($version | if length == 0 then [null] else . end | .[]) as $v |
                    ($language | if length == 0 then [null] else . end | .[]) as $l |
                    (
                        ({} |
                        (if $o != null then .os = $o else . end) |
                        (if $v != null then .version = $v else . end) |
                        (if $l != null then .language = $l else . end)
                        ) as $combo |

                        # Check exclusions
                        if ($exclude | any(.os as $eo | .version as $ev | .language as $el |
                            ($eo == null or ($combo.os // null) == $eo) and
                            ($ev == null or ($combo.version // null) == $ev) and
                            ($el == null or ($combo.language // null) == $el)
                        )) then
                            empty
                        else
                            $combo
                        end
                    )
                ]
            end
        ) as $cartesian |

        # Build result
        {include: ($cartesian + $include)} |

        # Validate size
        if ((.include | length) > $maxsize) then
            error("Matrix size \(.include | length) exceeds maximum \($maxsize)")
        else . end |

        # Add configuration options
        (if $failfast != null then .["fail-fast"] = $failfast else . end) |
        (if $maxparallel != null then .["max-parallel"] = $maxparallel else . end)
    ' <<< "$config") || {
        echo "Error: $(echo "$matrix" | tail -1)" >&2
        return 1
    }

    echo "$matrix"
}

main "$@"

name: GitHub Actions Matrix Generator

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main, master ]
    paths:
      - 'matrix-generator.sh'
      - 'tests/matrix-generator.bats'
      - '.github/workflows/environment-matrix-generator.yml'
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main, master ]
    paths:
      - 'matrix-generator.sh'
      - 'tests/matrix-generator.bats'
      - '.github/workflows/environment-matrix-generator.yml'
  workflow_dispatch:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 2 * * *'

jobs:
  test-matrix-generator:
    name: Test Matrix Generator
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          apt-get update
          apt-get install -y jq
          npm install -g bats

      - name: Make script executable
        run: chmod +x matrix-generator.sh

      - name: Validate script syntax
        run: bash -n matrix-generator.sh

      - name: Run shellcheck
        run: |
          apt-get install -y shellcheck || npm install -g shellcheck
          shellcheck matrix-generator.sh

      - name: Run bats tests
        run: bats tests/matrix-generator.bats

      - name: Test basic matrix generation
        run: |
          ./matrix-generator.sh '{"os": ["ubuntu-latest"], "version": ["1.0"]}' | jq .
          echo "Basic test passed"

      - name: Test cartesian product
        run: |
          result=$(./matrix-generator.sh '{"os": ["ubuntu", "macos"], "version": ["1", "2"]}')
          count=$(echo "$result" | jq '.include | length')
          if [ "$count" -eq 4 ]; then
            echo "Cartesian product test passed (4 combinations)"
          else
            echo "FAIL: Expected 4 combinations, got $count"
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Test include rules
        run: |
          result=$(./matrix-generator.sh '{"os": ["ubuntu"], "version": ["1"], "include": [{"os": "windows", "version": "2"}]}')
          echo "$result" | jq '.include[] | select(.os == "windows")' | grep -q windows
          echo "Include rules test passed"

      - name: Test exclude rules
        run: |
          result=$(./matrix-generator.sh '{"os": ["ubuntu", "macos"], "version": ["1", "2"], "exclude": [{"os": "macos", "version": "1"}]}')
          count=$(echo "$result" | jq '.include | length')
          if [ "$count" -eq 3 ]; then
            echo "Exclude rules test passed (3 combinations after excluding 1)"
          else
            echo "FAIL: Expected 3 combinations after exclusion, got $count"
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Test fail-fast configuration
        run: |
          result=$(./matrix-generator.sh '{"os": ["ubuntu"], "fail-fast": true}')
          failfast=$(echo "$result" | jq '.["fail-fast"]')
          if [ "$failfast" = "true" ]; then
            echo "Fail-fast test passed"
          else
            echo "FAIL: fail-fast not set correctly"
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Test max-parallel configuration
        run: |
          result=$(./matrix-generator.sh '{"os": ["ubuntu"], "max-parallel": 5}')
          maxpar=$(echo "$result" | jq '.["max-parallel"]')
          if [ "$maxpar" = "5" ]; then
            echo "Max-parallel test passed"
          else
            echo "FAIL: max-parallel not set correctly"
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Test matrix size validation
        run: |
          if ./matrix-generator.sh '{"os": ["u1", "u2", "u3"], "version": ["1", "2", "3"], "max-matrix-size": 5}' 2>&1 | grep -q "exceeds maximum"; then
            echo "Size validation test passed"
          else
            echo "FAIL: Size validation not working"
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Test error handling
        run: |
          if ./matrix-generator.sh '{"version": ["1"], "include": []}' 2>&1 | grep -q "required"; then
            echo "Error handling test passed"
          else
            echo "FAIL: Error handling not working"
            exit 1
          fi

  validate-workflow:
    name: Validate Workflow Configuration
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Install actionlint
        run: |
          apt-get update
          apt-get install -y golang-go
          go install github.com/rhysd/actionlint/cmd/actionlint@latest
          echo "$(go env GOPATH)/bin" >> $GITHUB_PATH

      - name: Validate workflow with actionlint
        run: actionlint .github/workflows/environment-matrix-generator.yml

    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          apt-get update
          apt-get install -y jq
          npm install -g bats

      - name: Make script executable
        run: chmod +x matrix-generator.sh

      - name: Validate script syntax
        run: bash -n matrix-generator.sh

      - name: Run shellcheck
        run: |

      - name: Run bats tests
        run: bats tests/matrix-generator.bats

      - name: Test basic matrix generation
        run: |
          ./matrix-generator.sh '{"os": ["ubuntu-latest"], "version": ["1.0"]}' | jq .

      - name: Test cartesian product
        run: |
          result=$(./matrix-generator.sh '{"os": ["ubuntu", "macos"], "version": ["1", "2"]}')
          count=$(echo "$result" | jq '.include | length')
          if [ "$count" -eq 4 ]; then
            echo "Cartesian product test passed (4 combinations)"
          else
            echo "FAIL: Expected 4 combinations, got $count"
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Test include rules
        run: |
          result=$(./matrix-generator.sh '{"os": ["ubuntu"], "version": ["1"], "include": [{"os": "windows", "version": "2"}]}')
          echo "$result" | jq '.include[] | select(.os == "windows")' | grep -q windows
          echo "Include rules test passed"

      - name: Test exclude rules
        run: |
          result=$(./matrix-generator.sh '{"os": ["ubuntu", "macos"], "version": ["1", "2"], "exclude": [{"os": "macos", "version": "1"}]}')
          count=$(echo "$result" | jq '.include | length')
          if [ "$count" -eq 3 ]; then

            exit 1

        run: |
          result=$(./matrix-generator.sh '{"os": ["ubuntu"], "fail-fast": true}')
          failfast=$(echo "$result" | jq '.["fail-fast"]')
          if [ "$failfast" = "true" ]; then
            echo "Fail-fast test passed"
          else
            echo "FAIL: fail-fast not set correctly"
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Test max-parallel configuration
        run: |
          result=$(./matrix-generator.sh '{"os": ["ubuntu"], "max-parallel": 5}')

The issue is with the Docker image permissions. The apt-get command is failing because of permission issues with the apt lists directory. This might be due to Docker running as a different user. Let me use a simpler workflow that doesn't try to install packages from apt-get, and instead relies on what's already available in the image. Let me also simplify by not trying to install actionlint in the act environment.

name: GitHub Actions Matrix Generator

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main, master ]
    paths:
      - 'matrix-generator.sh'
      - 'tests/matrix-generator.bats'
      - '.github/workflows/environment-matrix-generator.yml'
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main, master ]
    paths:
      - 'matrix-generator.sh'
      - 'tests/matrix-generator.bats'
      - '.github/workflows/environment-matrix-generator.yml'
  workflow_dispatch:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 2 * * *'

jobs:
  test-matrix-generator:
    name: Test Matrix Generator
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          sudo apt-get update
          sudo apt-get install -y jq
          npm install -g bats

      - name: Make script executable
        run: chmod +x matrix-generator.sh

      - name: Validate script syntax
        run: bash -n matrix-generator.sh

      - name: Run shellcheck
        run: |
          sudo apt-get install -y shellcheck
          shellcheck matrix-generator.sh

      - name: Run bats tests
        run: bats tests/matrix-generator.bats

      - name: Test basic matrix generation
        run: |
          ./matrix-generator.sh '{"os": ["ubuntu-latest"], "version": ["1.0"]}' | jq .
          echo "✓ Basic test passed"

      - name: Test cartesian product
        run: |
          result=$(./matrix-generator.sh '{"os": ["ubuntu", "macos"], "version": ["1", "2"]}')
          count=$(echo "$result" | jq '.include | length')
          if [ "$count" -eq 4 ]; then
            echo "✓ Cartesian product test passed (4 combinations)"
          else
            echo "✗ FAIL: Expected 4 combinations, got $count"
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Test include rules
        run: |
          result=$(./matrix-generator.sh '{"os": ["ubuntu"], "version": ["1"], "include": [{"os": "windows", "version": "2"}]}')
          echo "$result" | jq '.include[] | select(.os == "windows")' | grep -q windows
          echo "✓ Include rules test passed"

      - name: Test exclude rules
        run: |
          result=$(./matrix-generator.sh '{"os": ["ubuntu", "macos"], "version": ["1", "2"], "exclude": [{"os": "macos", "version": "1"}]}')
          count=$(echo "$result" | jq '.include | length')
          if [ "$count" -eq 3 ]; then
            echo "✓ Exclude rules test passed (3 combinations after excluding 1)"
          else
            echo "✗ FAIL: Expected 3 combinations after exclusion, got $count"
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Test fail-fast configuration
        run: |
          result=$(./matrix-generator.sh '{"os": ["ubuntu"], "fail-fast": true}')
          failfast=$(echo "$result" | jq '.["fail-fast"]')
          if [ "$failfast" = "true" ]; then
            echo "✓ Fail-fast test passed"
          else
            echo "✗ FAIL: fail-fast not set correctly"
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Test max-parallel configuration
        run: |
          result=$(./matrix-generator.sh '{"os": ["ubuntu"], "max-parallel": 5}')
          maxpar=$(echo "$result" | jq '.["max-parallel"]')
          if [ "$maxpar" = "5" ]; then
            echo "✓ Max-parallel test passed"
          else
            echo "✗ FAIL: max-parallel not set correctly"
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Test matrix size validation
        run: |
          if ./matrix-generator.sh '{"os": ["u1", "u2", "u3"], "version": ["1", "2", "3"], "max-matrix-size": 5}' 2>&1 | grep -q "exceeds maximum"; then
            echo "✓ Size validation test passed"
          else
            echo "✗ FAIL: Size validation not working"
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Test error handling
        run: |
          if ./matrix-generator.sh '{"version": ["1"], "include": []}' 2>&1 | grep -q "required"; then
            echo "✓ Error handling test passed"
          else
            echo "✗ FAIL: Error handling not working"
            exit 1
          fi

  validate-workflow:
    name: Validate Workflow Configuration
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Install actionlint
        run: |
          sudo apt-get update
          sudo apt-get install -y golang-go
          go install github.com/rhysd/actionlint/cmd/actionlint@latest
          echo "$(go env GOPATH)/bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"

      - name: Validate workflow with actionlint
        run: actionlint .github/workflows/environment-matrix-generator.yml
